baseline_test_note: Baseline not run during planning; executor should capture pre-change state of AgentChat.test.tsx before applying changes to distinguish pre-existing failures from regressions.
meta_commentary: All changes confined to reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx and its test file. Execute Step 2 (hardened optimistic identity) FIRST — it is a prerequisite for safe draining. Key gotchas: (1) Do NOT dequeue before sending — only slice queue head on success so failed items remain visible. (2) sendNow's catch block must clear optimisticMessage by id-match so drain effect isn't blocked, but error banner from useSendMessage still surfaces. (3) Optimistic-clear must use three signals (priorTurnCount growth + content match + timestamp >= sentAtMs - 1000ms skew) — duplicate text alone must not satisfy. (4) Verify AgentTurn.timestamp shape (ms vs ISO) at AgentChat.tsx:375-384 area before writing comparator; if ISO, use Date.parse. (5) Attachments snapshot at queue time — do NOT re-read clips at send time. (6) Voice path at AgentChat.tsx:174 already calls handleSend(text) so it queues naturally. (7) Use crypto.randomUUID() with fallback. (8) No hardcoded colors — use bg-muted/50 etc. (9) ChevronUp/ChevronDown from lucide-react. (10) Cancel button (AgentChat.tsx:522) and handleNewSession (AgentChat.tsx:419) and activeSessionId-change effect must all clear queue + optimistic state.
watch_items: FLAG-001: failed queued send must remain at queue head; sendNow catch must clear optimisticMessage by id so drain isn't permanently blocked; do NOT auto-retry.
FLAG-002: optimistic-clear must require turn count growth beyond priorTurnCount + content match + timestamp >= sentAtMs - 1000ms; duplicate-text messages must not prematurely clear.
Drain effect must NOT slice queue before awaiting sendNow — only slice on success (prev[0]?.id === next.id check guards reorder races).
Verify AgentTurn.timestamp is numeric ms; if ISO string, use Date.parse in comparator.
Attachments must be snapshotted at queue time, NOT re-read from clips at send time.
Cancel, handleNewSession, and activeSessionId change must all clear both queue and optimisticMessage.
Keep diff confined to AgentChat.tsx + AgentChat.test.tsx — no hook/server/DB changes.
Use design tokens (bg-muted, etc.) — no hardcoded colors per CLAUDE.md.
Existing optimistic-clear effect at AgentChat.tsx:375-384 is text-only and unsafe — must be rewritten, not just augmented.
useSendMessage error path (hooks/useAgentSession.ts:348-354) only sets localError; our finally must release sendingRef independently.
Reorder operates on tail items only — drain reads queue[0] at fire time so swapping lower items is race-free; never swap index 0 with -1.

sense_checks.question: Does the new OptimisticMessage type include id, text, attachments, sentAtMs, and priorTurnCount, and does the rewritten clear effect require turn count growth + content match + timestamp comparison (not text-only)?

Does sendNow snapshot priorTurnCount/sentAtMs at call time, clear optimisticMessage by id-match in catch, and always release sendingRef in finally? Does handleSend queue when busy (sendingRef || isProcessing || sendMessage.isPending || optimisticMessage || queue.length > 0) and snapshot attachments from current clips?
Does the drain effect await sendNow before slicing the queue head, use prev[0]?.id === next.id guard when slicing, leave failed items at head, and re-fire when optimisticMessage clears?

Are queued cards rendered above the clips strip with line-clamp-2 text, attachment count badge, ChevronUp (disabled at index 0), ChevronDown (disabled at last), and X delete — using design tokens not hardcoded colors?

Are isProcessing and sendMessage.isPending removed from input/send disabled conditions while keeping !hasTimeline/!activeSessionId/isCancelled/voice guards, and does the placeholder change to a queue-aware string when busy?
Do Cancel, handleNewSession, and the activeSessionId-change effect all clear both queue and optimisticMessage?

Do the new tests cover: typing-while-processing queues without calling mutateAsync; head auto-sends with snapshotted attachments after completion; FLAG-002 duplicate-text regression; FLAG-001 failed-send keeps head and pauses drain; reorder/delete behavior; deleting failed head resumes drain?
Did the executor run npm test -- AgentChat and confirm all tests pass, then write a throwaway script reproducing queue-while-processing behavior, run it, and delete it?

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Audit current send path / disabled gates / error path
Step 2: Add queue state, types, and hardened OptimisticMessage with rewritten clear effect
Step 3: Refactor handleSend and add sendNow with attachment snapshotting
Step 4: Auto-drain queue when idle, error-safe (no dequeue before send)
Step 5: Render queued-messages stack with reorder/delete controls
Step 6: Stop blocking input/send while sending; update placeholder
Step 7: Edge cases — Cancel, handleNewSession, session switch clear queue/optimistic
Step 8: Add tests for queueing, drain, FLAG-001/002 regressions, reorder/delete

validation.completeness_notes: Step 1 (audit) is folded into T1 since the audit informs the implementation in the same file — no separate inspect task needed per finalize guidance. All other plan steps map 1:1 to tasks.

tasks.description: In AgentChat.tsx, add QueuedMessage type and `queue` state next to draft/optimisticMessage (~line 139-141). Harden OptimisticMessage type to {id, text, attachments, sentAtMs, priorTurnCount}. Rewrite the optimistic-clear effect at AgentChat.tsx:375-384 so it clears only when activeSession.data.turns.length > optimistic.priorTurnCount AND there exists a user turn at index >= priorTurnCount whose content === optimistic.text AND whose timestamp (ms) >= optimistic.sentAtMs - 1000. Verify AgentTurn.timestamp shape; if ISO string, use Date.parse. Add id helper using crypto.randomUUID() with `${Date.now()}-${Math.random()}` fallback. Confirm gating sites at AgentChat.tsx:675 (input disabled), :715 (send disabled), :522 (cancel), :419-426 (new session) before later steps.

Extract `sendNow(item: QueuedMessage)` as useCallback with deps [sendMessage, clearGallerySelection, activeSessionId, timelineId, activeSession.data?.turns.length]. Body: set sendingRef.current=true, set optimisticMessage with priorTurnCount=activeSession.data?.turns.length ?? 0 and sentAtMs=Date.now(); try await sendMessage.mutateAsync({message:item.text, attachments:item.attachments}) then clearGallerySelection(); catch -> setOptimisticMessage(prev => prev && prev.id===item.id ? null : prev) (FLAG-001); finally release sendingRef. Rewrite handleSend(rawText?): trim text, snapshot attachments from current clips, clear draft only if rawText===undefined; if (sendingRef.current || isProcessing || sendMessage.isPending || optimisticMessage || queue.length > 0) append to queue and return; else call sendNow.

Add drain useEffect: early-return if sendingRef.current, isProcessing, sendMessage.isPending, optimisticMessage, queue.length===0, or no session. Otherwise grab `next = queue[0]` and run `void (async () => { try { await sendNow(next); setQueue(prev => prev[0]?.id === next.id ? prev.slice(1) : prev); } catch {} })()`. Do NOT slice before awaiting (FLAG-001). Deps: [queue, isProcessing, sendMessage.isPending, optimisticMessage, activeSessionId, timelineId, sendNow]. Add brief inline comment that failed head intentionally pauses drain until user removes it.

Render queued-messages stack immediately above the clips strip at AgentChat.tsx:615-628. For each queue item render a row with: text (line-clamp-2), attachment count badge (📎 N) when attachments.length>0, ChevronUp button (disabled at index 0), ChevronDown button (disabled at last index), X delete button. Handlers: moveUp/moveDown swap adjacent items in a fresh array; remove(id) filters by id. Use bg-muted/50 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-xs flex items-center gap-2 — no hardcoded colors. Import ChevronUp/ChevronDown from lucide-react if not already imported.

Remove `isProcessing` from input disabled at AgentChat.tsx:675 (keep !hasTimeline, !activeSessionId, isCancelled, voice.isRecording, voice.isProcessing). Remove `isProcessing` and `sendMessage.isPending` from send-button disabled at AgentChat.tsx:715 (keep !draft.trim() and other guards). Update placeholder at AgentChat.tsx:673 to show 'Type to queue next message…' (or similar) when isProcessing || sendMessage.isPending || queue.length > 0.

Wire cleanup paths. Cancel handler at AgentChat.tsx:522: also setQueue([]) and setOptimisticMessage(null). handleNewSession at AgentChat.tsx:419-426: setQueue([]) (and clear optimistic). Add useEffect(() => { setQueue([]); setOptimisticMessage(null); }, [activeSessionId]) to clear on session switch.

In AgentChat.test.tsx, read existing patterns for mocking useSendMessage/useAgentSession then add tests: (a) Typing while isProcessing=true is allowed; Enter pushes onto visible queue and does NOT call sendMessage.mutateAsync. (b) When isProcessing flips false and optimistic clears, head triggers mutateAsync with attachments captured at queue time (not current clips). (c) FLAG-002 regression: two queued items with identical text — after first resolves, optimistic clears only when new user turn (index beyond priorTurnCount, timestamp >= sentAtMs) appears, not against stale same-text turn. (d) FLAG-001 regression: when mutateAsync rejects, head item remains in queue, drain pauses, error banner surfaces. (e) Reorder (up/down) and delete update rendered list; deleting failed head resumes draining.

Run `npm test -- AgentChat` from reigh-app/. Fix any failures by editing the code (not tests) and re-run until green. Then write a throwaway script (e.g. /tmp/verify-queue.md or a short scratch test) that mentally walks through the queue-while-processing scenario including duplicate-text and forced-error cases — actually exercise via the dev server if feasible — confirm correct behavior, then delete the scratch artifact. Run repo-wide typecheck/lint as final check.
